div.span: RAID 6 - Striping with Double Parity

Disk 1
---
Block A1
Parity BP
Parity CQ

Disk 2
---
Block A2
Block B1
Parity CP

Disk 3
---
Parity AP
Block B2
Block C1

Disk 4
---
Parity AQ
Parity BQ
Block C2

⚡ Performance: Moderate
💾 Capacity: (n-2)/n
🛡️ Fault Tolerance: 2 disks
✅ Min 4 disks required
RAID 5 - Striping with Parity

Disk 1
---
Block A1
Block B2
Parity C

Disk 2
---
Block A2
Parity B
Block C1

Disk 3
---
Parity A
Block B1
Block C2

⚡ Performance: Good
💾 Capacity: (n-1)/n
🛡️ Fault Tolerance: 1 disk
✅ Min 3 disks required
RAID 1 - Mirroring

Disk 1
---
Block A
Block B
Block C
🔄 Mirror

Disk 2
---
Block A
Block B
Block C

⚡ Performance: Read Fast
💾 Capacity: 50%
🛡️ Fault Tolerance: 1 disk
✅ Complete data redundancy
RAID 0 - Striping (No Redundancy)

Disk 1
---
Block A1
Block A3
Block A5

Disk 2
---
Block A2
Block A4
Block A6

⚡ Performance: Excellent
💾 Capacity: 100%
🛡️ Fault Tolerance: NONE
❌ Any disk failure = data loss

📊 RAID Selection Guide
----------------
RAID 0: Maximum performance, no safety
RAID 1: Maximum safety, 50% capacity
RAID 5: Balanced (common for servers)
RAID 6: High safety (critical systems)
